scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text:
B

Both Alan's PR (#312) and Sarah's PR (#313) alter the events table (Alan adds webhook_status and delivery_attempts; Sarah adds delivery_state and processor_version).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. The workspace shows Sarah is a part-time contractor who only works Monday/Wednesday/Friday, so she’s unavailable for a Thursday (March 13) meeting.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B — SAML SSO. Josh told Acme it would come after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly scoped SAML out of this phase (“No SAML in this phase — ... would add 3 weeks”) and the ship notes only mention JWT + OAuth2 (Google/GitHub).

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B) The new flow removed 4 steps (including "first milestone"), so "completion" now means a shorter, different funnel — the metric isn't measuring the same outcome as before.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. The workspace notes staging-3 was created for load testing (last deploy Jan 22) but was "never actually used for load testing."

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — each ticket lists "Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25," pointing to a common migration as the likely root cause.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: B — The design-doc summary explicitly lists two open questions: pagination (cursor vs offset) and the webhook payload format (nested vs flat).

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace says the migration script that maps plan/tier/subscription is "the rosetta stone file (src/billing/mapping.rs)".

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B — CSV only. Customer research shows all three prospects said CSV is sufficient (Acme: import to QuickBooks; Pinnacle: monthly reconciliation; Waverly: CSV works), no one requested PDF, and the Excel ask came from sales, not the customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace test distribution lists 0 edge case tests (0%) and 7 end-to-end tests (2%), with 280 unit (82%) and 55 integration (16%) tests.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text:
B

Sarah's synthesis: "You can have JWT tokens that feel like API keys with long-lived tokens and bearer auth" — i.e., use long-lived JWTs that offer JWT capabilities while providing API-key–like simplicity for developers.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The plan explicitly calls out "No delivery confirmation mechanism" meaning a 200 from the customer's endpoint could be a false positive; neither side would detect unprocessed events until invoices don't reconcile weeks later.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B — Takeshi at NovaTech. The notes say the customer perspective hasn't been asked yet and Takeshi offered to review the proposal (responds within ~2 hours), so we should loop him in.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace totals ~2.75 hours/week: 1h (hardcoded URLs) + 45m (retry logic) + 1h (generic error messages); the skipped test adds missed-bug risk but no extra weekly time estimate.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc) asked in the last two weeks; Sarah already has a signature module so adding Ed25519 is localized and should take on the order of half a day.

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C — Break-even at feature 5. Calculation: without refactor = 5 days/feature ⇒ 5×5 = 25 days. With refactor = 3 weeks (15 days) + 2 days/feature ⇒ 15 + 2×5 = 25 days. Net positive only from feature 6 onward.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The root cause is a spec ambiguity: "subscription start date" was interpreted as billing-period start (Feb 1) by engineering while Acme expects the original signup date (Jan 15). The workspace notes this is a spec issue and can be resolved with a 1-line UI label change or showing both dates.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B — Adding annual billing would expand billing logic (proration/refunds/dunning) and thus directly impacts the Billing Migration schedule. Phase 2 must ship Mar 31 and is already blocked on ticket #847 with a Mar 15 reconciliation risk and no buffer, so scope creep would conflict with that launch.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring detected it in 12 minutes (alert at 09:12) and the root cause was a process gap: the migration wasn't tested against production-scale data with full historical records (legacy accounts missing plan_change_date).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec, which is 95% of our 100 req/sec limit. With typical 1.5–2× spikes that becomes ~143–190 req/sec, exceeding the limit and causing 429 errors (per workspace notes).
